title: salix × hermaphroditica
intro: Salix × hermaphroditica, also known as hybrid willow, is a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the goat willow (Salix caprea), resulting in a unique plant with combined characteristics of both parents. The medicinal properties of Salix × hermaphroditica are attributed to its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to reduce inflammation and pain. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and headaches, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Salix × hermaphroditica can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
